tools/migrate/viewgen.purpose: Standalone code generator that produces tools/migrate/views.sql by extracting Ent view schemas (ent.View) from openmeter/ent/schema/; bridges the Atlas gap where Ent view entities do not appear in migrate.Tables and are therefore invisible to atlas migrate diff.

patterns.name: GenerateSQL vs GenerateFile split
patterns.description: GenerateSQL returns []byte (testable, no I/O); GenerateFile calls GenerateSQL then writes to disk. Callers that need in-memory SQL for testing use GenerateSQL directly.

patterns.name: Schema-driven view collection via entc/load
patterns.description: CollectViews uses load.Config{Path}.Load() to read all Ent schemas, filters s.View==true, then reads entsql.Annotation for ViewFor[dialectName] falling back to ViewAs. Schemas with neither annotation are silently skipped.

patterns.name: Double JSON round-trip for EntSQL annotation
patterns.description: Ent's load package deserialises annotations as map[string]any; entSQLAnnotation marshals the raw value back to JSON then unmarshals into *entsql.Annotation to get typed fields.

patterns.name: View name derivation with three-level fallback
patterns.description: View name is resolved as: ant.Table -> s.Config.Table -> strcase.CamelToSnake(inflect.Pluralize(s.Name)). Verify the derived name matches the expected Postgres view name before regenerating.
patterns.example: name := ant.Table; if name == "" { name = s.Config.Table }; if name == "" { name = strcase.CamelToSnake(inflect.Pluralize(s.Name)) }

patterns.name: Alphabetic sort before render
patterns.description: Views are sorted by Name before RenderSQL so output is deterministic and PR diffs are stable across regenerations.
patterns.example: sort.Slice(views, func(i, j int) bool { return views[i].Name < views[j].Name })

patterns.name: Generated-file header
patterns.description: RenderSQL always prepends '-- Code generated by viewgen, DO NOT EDIT.' and '-- Regenerate with: make generate-view-sql' so consumers know not to hand-edit views.sql.
patterns.example: buf.WriteString("-- Code generated by viewgen, DO NOT EDIT.\n")

key_file_guides.role: Entire package — loads Ent schemas via entc/load, collects ViewDef structs, renders CREATE VIEW SQL with deterministic ordering.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new view schema using neither ViewFor nor ViewAs is silently skipped with no error. If ant.Table and s.Config.Table are both empty the name is derived from the Go struct name — verify it matches the expected Postgres view name. Only dialect.Postgres is passed today; multi-dialect support requires updating CollectViews callers.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing tools/migrate/views.sql — it is generated; run 'make generate-view-sql' instead.
Adding view DDL directly to Atlas migration files — Atlas does not diff Ent views; viewgen is the only correct path.
Relying on 'atlas migrate --env local diff' to pick up a new ent.View schema — Atlas reports no changes for view entities.
Skipping the alphabetic sort when adding new render logic — non-deterministic output breaks PR diffs.
Calling entSQLAnnotation on non-view schemas — the function is only meaningful when s.View==true; calling it elsewhere wastes cycles and may silently produce nil.

decisions.decision: Separate viewgen tool instead of an inline Atlas hook
decisions.rationale: Ent's Atlas integration does not surface view schemas in migrate.Tables, so a standalone loader (entc/load) is required to extract ViewFor/ViewAs annotations and produce DDL outside the normal migration pipeline.
Allows schemas to provide Postgres-specific view SQL while keeping a generic fallback, supporting future multi-dialect view definitions without changing the generator interface.

decisions.decision: GenerateSQL returns bytes, GenerateFile handles I/O
decisions.rationale: Separating SQL generation from file I/O keeps the core logic unit-testable without touching the filesystem, while GenerateFile provides the convenient single-call entry point for the Makefile target.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new Ent view schema and regenerate views.sql
